title: Red Wine on Polyester
timeEstimate: 20–40 minutes; cold + Dawn + peroxide standard tannin protocol
stainChemistry: Red wine = anthocyanin pigments + condensed tannins. On polyester, tannins bond through grip. Polyester tolerates standard tannin chemistry (cold + Dawn + 3% peroxide) well — it doesn't shrink, doesn't set at moderate temperatures, doesn't bleed dye easily. The only polyester-specific caution: heat over time yellows polyester, so hot dryer + residual wine = permanent yellow shadow on the fiber even after the wine itself is gone. Tannin rules apply: NO alkaline cleaners (OxiClean, baking soda) — turn anthocyanin permanently darker.
whyThisWorks: Cold water + Dawn lifts fresh anthocyanin. 3% peroxide oxidizes residual tannin. Vinegar keeps anthocyanin on its lighter (red, acid-side) form rather than drifting to dark blue-purple. Polyester is the easy fiber for red wine — tolerates all the standard tannin tools. Heat is the only invariant.
defaultAssumption: Polyester garment / blend / workout wear / bedding. Fresh red wine = quick win. Hot-dried = permanent. Polyester at home is one of the more forgiving wine surfaces.

spottingProtocol.instruction: Flip inside out. Run cold water through the BACK of the stain. Cold pulls fresh anthocyanin out the way it came in.
Work Dawn into the spot with a soft toothbrush. Cool rinse. Skip OxiClean — alkaline darkens tannin.
Hidden-area test if dyed polyester. 3% peroxide damp cloth 5–10 min. Don't mix with bleach / ammonia / alkaline.
Vinegar rinse 5 min. Neutralizes peroxide + keeps anthocyanin on the red (light) side rather than drifting blue-purple.
Cool wash per care label — NOT hot. Air-dry. Inspect BEFORE dryer — heat permanently sets residual wine on polyester.

steps: 5. Cool wash + air-dry. Inspect before dryer.
professionalProtocol.temperature: cold throughout — heat sets tannin + yellows polyester
warnings: NEVER chlorine bleach on polyester — yellows fiber
NEVER hot dryer until fully clear — heat permanent
Hidden-area peroxide test on dyed polyester

homeSolutions.agent: Polyester Is the Easy Fiber for Red Wine
homeSolutions.instruction: Among the fibers, polyester is one of the easier surfaces for red wine. The standard tannin protocol (cold water + Dawn + 3% peroxide + vinegar + cool wash) works directly. Two rules: (1) NO alkaline cleaners (OxiClean, baking soda) — they turn wine tannin DARKER permanently, and (2) NO hot water / hot dryer until the stain is fully gone — heat sets wine + yellows polyester.
Flip the garment inside out. Run cold tap water through the BACK of the stain for 1–2 minutes. The most effective fast move.
Work a drop of Dawn into the wet stain with a soft toothbrush 5 minutes. Cool rinse.
Dab 3% drugstore peroxide on the spot 5–10 minutes. Hidden-area test on dyed polyester first. Don't mix with bleach / ammonia / OxiClean.
1 tablespoon white vinegar + 1 cup cool water. 5 minutes. Neutralizes peroxide + keeps any residual anthocyanin in its lighter (red) form.
Wash on COOL — NOT hot. Air-dry. Inspect BEFORE dryer. ANY residual wine + dryer heat = permanent on polyester. Once you can confirm fully clear in daylight, normal dryer is fine.

steps: 1. NO alkaline cleaners. NO hot water until fully clear.
4. 3% peroxide damp cloth, 5–10 min, hidden test on dyed.
6. Cool wash + AIR-DRY. Inspect before dryer.
diyProtocol.whenToStop: Stop if hot dryer was used on residual wine (likely permanent), if alkaline was applied (tannin darkened), or if the polyester is delicate-finish (chiffon-style).
warnings: NEVER hot water on red wine
NEVER chlorine bleach on polyester — yellows fiber
NEVER hot dryer until fully clear
NEVER mix peroxide with bleach / ammonia

neverDo: Never hot water on red wine
Never OxiClean / baking soda / ammonia on tannin
Never hot dryer on residual wine
Never mix peroxide with bleach / ammonia
fiberSensitivities: Polyester tolerates 3% peroxide on dyed (with hidden test)
Polyester yellows under heat over time
Polyester doesn't shrink at moderate temperatures

materialWarnings: No hot water — sets wine + yellows polyester
Polyester is the forgiving fiber for red wine

escalation.when: Hot dryer used on residual wine, alkaline cleaner applied, set-in stain weeks old with heat, or delicate polyester.
escalation.whatToTell: "Red wine on polyester, age [hours / days], home-treated with cold + Dawn + 3% peroxide + vinegar + cool wash. No hot water / no alkaline. [Residual / hot-dried]."

contextQuestions: How long ago — fresh / overnight / older?
Has it been through a hot wash or dryer?
deepSolveHooks.modifierNotes: Polyester is one of the easier fibers for red wine. Hot-dried = permanent. Pre-applied OxiClean = tannin darkened permanently. Delicate polyester = closer to silk protocol.
